title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Americas — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: The Americas faces synchronized federal and state-level crypto compliance infrastructure buildout through September 2026. The United States dominates with 16 events—FinCEN's foundational BSA/AML reform comment period closes June 9, GENIUS Act stablecoin issuer AML frameworks enter 60-day comment windows, and multiple states advance VASP licensing regimes. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: Twenty-one EU jurisdictions execute MiCA Level 2 standards while AMLA becomes operational with direct supervision authority over highest-risk CASPs. UK's FCA sets hard authorization deadlines for existing crypto businesses, forcing firms to submit applications or exit. Middle East regulators finalize licensing frameworks across Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Israel, while France's July 1 PSAN-to-MiCA transition creates immediate compliance infrastructure demand.

title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: Nine Asia-Pacific jurisdictions are executing simultaneous VASP licensing, institutional custody standards, and AML enforcement through September 2026. Australia transitions from Travel Rule implementation to active enforcement while consulting on stablecoin payment licensing—the first major market to regulate crypto payment gateways as PSPs.
